mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean 20-day forward return is negative during periods of rising PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, the Federal Reserve maintains exceptionally accommodative monetary policy that suppresses short-term discount rates and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate demand expansion coupled with labor market tightening, which jointly lift corporate revenue expectations more than labor costs and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where RSAFS rises month-over-month and UNRATE falls, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term contributes no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During the zero lower bound regime, an increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signals rising nominal revenues that outpace financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, rising CPIAUCSL coincides with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above 900 thousand units signals durable construction demand and household formation strength, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST exceeds 900.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets and accelerating wage pressures that compress aggregate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals rising manufacturing output and inventory investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO correspond with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal supply expansion matched by labor absorption, jointly boosting capacity utilization and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.
